title: DNA Extraction from Whatman Filter Papers
author: Xiang Guo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vjme4k6

steps.title: Filter paper disc containing blood cut and placed into 1
steps.instruction: Filter paper disc containing blood cut and placed into 1.5 mL EP tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000 × g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000 × g for 00:05:00 , discard the supernatant with a micropipettor. go to step #2 until the DNA precipitate forms a white or pink gel-like pellet at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Measure absorbance at 230nm, 260nm, and...
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at 230nm, 260nm, and 280nm, Calculate the A260/A280 and A230/A260 ratio.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 150 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 150 µL 10% Chelex-100 solution，vortex the sample briefly for 00:00:30 . Chelex 100 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C7901-100G

steps.title: Add 1 mL RNase-free water and vortex to mix
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL RNase-free water and vortex to mix. DNAse/RNAse free distilled water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10977023

steps.title: Incubate at 25 °C, Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 25 °C, Room temperature for 00:15:00 to lyse RBC.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the supernatant to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the supernatant to a clean 1.5 mL EP tube with a micropipettor. Proceed to downstream applications, or store the DNA at -20 °C .
